claim.observation: The safe summary records 60,371 EVTX rows across Security, Application, OAlerts, and System logs; authentication and service/process event counts; and an exported SRUM set where the network table has 4 rows with zero total sent/received bytes, while the SRUM id map contains application-path rows matching browser/cloud/P2P/messaging needles.
claim.interpretation: EVTX now supplies authentication, process-creation, privilege, failed-logon, and service-install follow-up leads. The SRUM network table from this export is not a strong network-byte witness because the selected network table has only four zero-byte rows; the SRUM id map is useful as application-path context, not proof of file-specific network transfer or exfiltration.
